facts.claim: 33 rules cover the vital-sign/lab collection surface: FE plausibility bounds on 3 forms (movimentacao, enfermagem, formulario_medico) mirrored by ~24 backend field validators in utils/validators.py plus dados_prontuario.py model-field wiring, and a small SinaisVitais viewset (list/destroy/manage_data) with no clinical scoring formula in-cluster.

Cluster is purely input-plausibility validation, not scoring: bounded fields (PaO2/PaCO2/lactate/bilirubin/creatinine/platelets/leukocytes/tidal-volume/PEEP/PINS) feed downstream SOFA, sepsis-severity, SIRS and ventilator criteria whose formulas live in other clusters (sepse, ventilacao).

Zero-value sentinel ('not measured') is applied inconsistently across structurally similar validators: PAS exempts 0, PAD does not; FiO2 exempts 0, structurally-identical SatO2 does not; Temperatura and TEC and Glasgow all exempt 0; FR, PaO2, PaCO2, lactate, bilirubin, creatinine, leukocytes, platelets, tidal-volume, PEEP, PINS, dobutamine, noradrenaline, sedativo have no zero exemption.

Two validators are dead code: PAMValidator (0-200) is defined but its model-field application and import are commented out (dados_prontuario.py:84-85), so PAM is never stored/validated; SatO2Validator (21-100) is likewise defined but disabled on the model field, so SpO2 is unvalidated at runtime.

Frontend/backend divergence: the physician form (formulario_medico) leaves heart rate, respiratory rate, temperature and SpO2 UNBOUNDED while the movimentacao form and backend validators enforce HR 0-200, FR 0-50, temp 20-43; PAS/PAD bounds match across all surfaces (50-250 / 0-150).

Capillary refill time (TEC) is encoded three incompatible ways across forms - numeric 3-20s (movimentacao, matches backend TecValidator), boolean '>5s?' (enfermagem), checkbox 'TEC > 5s' (formulario_medico) - and the numeric floor of 3s excludes physiologically-normal refill, conflicting with the published <=2-3s normal cutoff (ANDROMEDA-SHOCK, JAMA 2019).

Histogram (n=33): status OK=28/DISCREPANCY=4/AMBIGUOUS=1; type threshold=5/validation=26/workflow=2; category alert-threshold=5/data-validation=24/care-pathway=1/clinical-scoring=1/drug-dosing=3 (approx, workflow rules 007-008 categorized care-pathway/data-validation respectively); verification verdict NOT_APPLICABLE=28/VERIFIED=1/DISCREPANCY=2/UNVERIFIABLE=2.

Vasoactive/sedative dosing validators have no clinically-anchored unit: DobutaminaValidator 0-30 and NoradrenalinaValidator 0-200 have units unstated in code (guessed ml/h vs mcg/kg/min across captures); both marked verify=true / UNVERIFIABLE against published mcg/kg/min dosing (dobutamine 2-20, norepinephrine 0.05-0.4) because the raw numeric bound cannot be mapped to a real-world dose without knowing the unit.

constraints.text: MUST NOT port PAMValidator or SatO2Validator disabled-state silently — v2 must decide (RATIFY) whether MAP and SpO2 are validated fields at all; if adopted, correct the SatO2/FiO2 zero-exemption asymmetry (ADOPT-CORRECTED).

MUST unify capillary-refill capture to one representation (recommend boolean/ordinal at the >2-3s clinical cutoff per ANDROMEDA-SHOCK) instead of porting three divergent FE encodings or the non-physiological 3s numeric floor.
MUST require an explicit unit (mcg/kg/min) for any vasoactive/sedative dosing field carried into v2; the legacy 0-30/0-200 bounds are unit-less and UNVERIFIABLE as clinical dose limits.
MUST fix the physician-form under-validation (HR/FR/temp/SpO2 unbounded) so all vital-sign entry surfaces enforce the same bounds as the backend, avoiding accept-then-reject UX.

MUST use the non-deleted manager for any v2 vitals listing endpoint; legacy SinaisVitaisViewSet.get_queryset() uses the default manager and leaks soft-deleted rows (same bug pattern as balanco-hidrico entrada/saida).

open_questions: What real-world unit (ml/h vs mcg/kg/min) the legacy dobutamina/noradrenalina/sedativo dosing fields actually represent — unresolved in source, blocks porting numeric bounds as clinical dose limits.
Whether PAM and SpO2 validation was intentionally disabled (product decision) or an unfinished migration — affects whether v2 should validate these fields at all.
